 COOKING INSTRUCTIONS:
For best results thaw frozen pierogi before preparing. Cook Thoroughly-For food safety and quality. Heat to a minimum internal temperature of 165° F.
Do Not Re-freeze.
 FOR BEST QUALITY: If frozen, thaw Pierogi. Preheat skillet with 4-5 tbsp. of butter or oil. Place Pierogi in a skillet and reduce heat to low. Turn occasionally until golden brown. IMPORTANT: HIGH HEAT TOUGHENS THE DOUGH
 To Boil: Heat 3 to 4 quarts of water to boil (add 1 tbsp of salt, if desired). Add Pierogi to pot, stir, and return to boil. Reduce heat to medium and cook 1 to 2 minutes if fresh or 2 to 3 minutes if frozen, or when they rise to the top. Drain. IMPORTANT: Overcooking decreases qr ty.

 TRADITIONAL METHOD:
1. Bring 3/4 cup of water to a boil. 2. Add 1/4 cup Quick Cook Steel Cut Oats. 3. Reduce heat to low, simmer uncovered for 5-7 minutes, stirring regularly. 4. Remove from heat and allow to stand for 1 minute before serving. MICROWAVE DIRECTIONS: 1. Add 34 cup of boiling water to ¼ cup Quick Cook Steel Cut Oats in a deep microwave-safe bowl (leaving room in the bowl for the oats to expand
during cooking).
2. Cook on High for 3 minutes, stir and cook for a further 2-3 minutes. 3. Remove from the microwave and allow to stand for 1 minute before serving. For thicker oatmeal use less water. For thinner oatmeal use more water.

 TO MICROWAVE: Allow 70g rice per person. Add the rice to a large microwavable bowl with 500ml water per 70g rice and stir once. Cover and vent. Cook on full power for 20 minutes (800W).
